title: Organic milk shortage — supplier missed 2 deliveries
description: Horizon Organic 64oz whole milk (SKU-4411) has been out of stock for 3 days. Supplier (DairyFresh Co) missed scheduled deliveries on 4/10 and 4/12. Customers are complaining — we sell ~120 units/week. Checked with neighboring store (Elm Street) and they are also affected.

title: Same organic milk supplier delay — DairyFresh Co
description: Confirming same issue as Greenfield Plaza. DairyFresh Co no-showed twice. We have 8 units left, maybe 1 day of stock. Need emergency substitute supplier authorization.

title: 3 call-outs on Saturday — cannot cover floor
description: Three associates called out for Saturday shift (busiest day). Only 4 of 7 scheduled showed up. Bakery and deli sections are unstaffed. Overtime approval needed for remaining team + requesting emergency temp staffing.

title: Walk-in cooler temperature alarm — perishables at risk
description: Walk-in cooler #2 temperature reading 52°F (should be 35-38°F). Compressor making unusual noise. ~$8K worth of dairy and produce at risk. Called HVAC vendor (CoolTech) — earliest availability is Monday. Need emergency vendor authorization.

title: Health inspection scheduled for next week — prep checklist incomplete
description: County health inspector visit confirmed for 4/18. Three checklist items still open: (1) employee handwashing log gaps for 4/8-4/10, (2) thermometer calibration overdue in deli, (3) pest control service report from March not filed. Need guidance on priority and any regional support.

title: Planogram reset delayed — seasonal endcap transition
description: Spring seasonal endcap transition was due 4/7 but we're still showing winter clearance. Team hasn't had bandwidth due to inventory counts. Requesting 3-day extension or regional merchandising support.

title: Customer slip-and-fall incident — legal documentation needed
description: Customer reported a slip near produce wet floor area at 2:15 PM. Incident report filed. Customer requested manager contact info and mentioned contacting attorney. Security camera footage preserved. Need guidance on corporate legal notification timeline.

title: Seasonal allergy medicine display empty — vendor managed
description: Claritin and Zyrtec vendor-managed display is empty. Vendor (Johnson Distribution) usually restocks Wednesdays. Last restock was skipped. Not urgent but losing sales — allergy season peak.

title: New hire onboarding delayed — POS training backlog
description: Two new hires started 4/7 but still can't operate registers. Training module system was down 4/7-4/9. They're bagging only. Need them register-certified before weekend rush.

title: Organized retail theft — cosmetics section hit 3 times this week
description: Third incident of coordinated grab-and-run in cosmetics aisle. Estimated $1,200 in losses this week alone. LP camera caught 2 individuals. Filed police reports (#PR-2026-4412, #PR-2026-4418). Requesting anti-theft fixture budget approval.

title: Self-checkout lane 3 and 5 error loops — customer frustration
description: Two of six self-checkout lanes throwing card reader errors intermittently. Customers abandoning SCO for staffed lanes, creating long lines. IT ticket #IT-88421 open since 4/8, no response yet.

title: Warehouse delivery 6 hours late — fresh produce wilting on truck
description: Scheduled 5AM delivery arrived at 11AM. Truck refrigeration was marginal. Lettuce, berries, and cut fruit look borderline. Need quality inspection guidance — accept partial, reject all, or document and accept with discount claim?

title: Online order pickup — 4 orders unfulfillable due to out-of-stock
description: Four BOPIS (buy online pick up in store) orders can't be fulfilled — items show in-stock in system but shelf is empty. Customers arriving expecting orders. System inventory vs physical count mismatch. Need process for customer communication and system correction.

title: Fire exit blocked by delivery pallets — code violation
description: Morning delivery crew left 4 pallets blocking rear fire exit. Discovered during opening walk. Cleared immediately but this is the second time in 30 days. Need formal vendor warning + documentation for fire marshal compliance file.
resolution_notes: Pallets cleared in 45 min. Vendor (QuickShip Logistics) issued written warning. Photo documentation filed. Added to delivery SOP checklist.

title: Employee requesting ADA accommodation — ergonomic equipment
description: Long-term associate (12 years) submitted ADA accommodation request for standing desk mat and modified lifting restrictions. Need HR guidance on approval process and timeline. Employee is in good standing.

title: Competitor opened across the street — pricing intelligence needed
description: New discount grocery (FreshMart) opened 4/5 across the street. Anecdotally seeing 10-15% foot traffic drop. Team did informal price comparison on 20 staples — we're 8-22% higher on 14 items. Requesting competitive response guidance.

title: Shopping cart corral — 12 carts with broken wheels
description: Accumulated 12 carts with locked/broken wheels in the corral. Customers grabbing them and returning immediately. Cart vendor contract allows batch replacement but minimum is 20. Requesting approval to add to next order or find interim solution.

title: Regional pattern: DairyFresh Co affecting 4+ stores — vendor review
description: I'm seeing DairyFresh Co delivery failures across Greenfield (ISS-001), Elm Street (ISS-002), and reports from 2 stores in adjacent region. This is a systemic vendor issue. Initiating 30-day vendor performance review. Need: (1) backup supplier list for organic dairy, (2) penalty clause from DairyFresh contract, (3) customer communication template for affected SKUs.

title: Q2 shrinkage trending 15% above target — action plan needed
description: Regional shrinkage report shows NE region at 2.3% vs 2.0% target. Lakeside (STR-107) is the outlier at 3.1%. Requesting: (1) store-by-store shrinkage breakdown, (2) LP audit schedule acceleration for top 3 stores, (3) comparison to other regions.

title: Summer hiring pipeline — seasonal staffing forecast
description: Summer volume increase starts mid-May. Historical data shows we need 40-50 seasonal hires across 8 stores. Last year we started recruiting 4/15 and still had gaps in June. Requesting: (1) store-by-store headcount forecast, (2) job fair coordination, (3) training schedule template.
